title: Inter-agent message sanitization: block lateral trust propagation via notes_inbox/notes_outbox
description: Add a prompt-injection and trust-manipulation detection pass on messages flowing through the notes_inbox/notes_outbox pipeline between agents. Currently, the skill audit (soul_manager.audit_skill()) checks imported skill files for malicious code, but there is no equivalent check on inter-agent messages. The explorer agent fetches external content (arxiv, reddit, HF feeds) and writes summaries into notes_outbox; the super agent reads those and dispatches to writer, socialmedia, and general agents. This is a concrete lateral agent-to-agent trust propagation path: a poisoned feed entry could carry a prompt injection that travels explorer → super → writer → Substack publish. The fix: in notes_bridge.py (or wherever the super agent reads outbox messages), add a lightweight sanitization step that flags messages containing injection-pattern strings (e.g., 'ignore previous instructions', 'system:', role-override patterns, base64 blobs above a threshold length) and quarantines rather than dispatches them.
rationale: The reading note's central claim is that legitimate trust relationships — not malicious code — are the actual attack surface in multi-agent systems. Thompson's framework looks for 'attacker vs. defender'; the four-layer taxonomy says the lateral agent-to-agent channel IS the attack vector. Mira's architecture instantiates this exactly: explorer has external network access and writes into a shared message bus that other agents consume without re-validation. The skill audit protects the code layer but leaves the data/message layer unguarded. The reading note's unanswered question ('can we make the lateral attack concrete?') is answered by Mira's own pipeline: explorer fetches → notes_outbox → super dispatches → writer publishes to Substack. Each arrow is an unvalidated trust handoff.
diff_description: In notes_bridge.py, add a function `sanitize_agent_message(msg: str, source_agent: str) -> tuple[str, bool]` that: (1) scans for a hardcoded list of injection patterns (case-insensitive regex: 'ignore (all |previous |prior )?(instructions|rules|constraints)', 'system prompt', '<\/?(system|assistant|user)>', base64 strings >200 chars); (2) returns (msg, True) if clean, (msg, False) if flagged; (3) logs flagged messages to logs/injection_quarantine.jsonl with timestamp, source_agent, and matched pattern. In super/core.py, wrap the outbox read loop to call sanitize_agent_message() on each message from externally-connected agents (explorer, socialmedia, surfer) and skip dispatch + emit a warning log if flagged=False. Do not apply to internal agents (writer, researcher, coder) whose inputs are user-originated or Mira-generated only.
